historical_cases.name: 2021-2022 Lithium Price Supercycle
historical_cases.peak_move: Lithium carbonate spot prices rose approximately 10x from early 2021 to late 2022; upstream miners (Albemarle, SQM, Ganfeng) observed multi-hundred-percent equity gains while EV OEM margins compressed visibly
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Demand slowdown in China EV market combined with new spodumene and brine project supply coming online; lithium prices collapsed roughly 80% from peak through 2023

historical_cases.name: 2022-2023 IRA-Driven Battery Supply Chain Reshoring
historical_cases.approximate_duration: 约 12-18 个月 (initial capital allocation wave)
historical_cases.peak_move: Announced gigafactory investments in the US exceeded $50B in 2022-2023; battery materials stocks (Livent, MP Materials, Piedmont Lithium) saw pronounced re-rating; Korean cell makers (LG Energy, Samsung SDI) entered US JV structures
historical_cases.exit_trigger: EV demand deceleration in late 2023 caused OEMs (Ford, GM) to pause or scale back gigafactory commitments; IRA credit uncertainty under 2024 political cycle added hesitation

historical_cases.name: 2018-2019 US-China Tariff Shock on EV Components
historical_cases.peak_move: Section 301 tariffs on Chinese EV battery cells and components prompted visible supply chain re-routing discussions; Chinese battery makers (CATL) accelerated non-US geographic diversification; US-listed pure-play Chinese EV names (NIO, Xpeng) saw elevated volatility correlated with tariff escalation headlines
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Phase One trade deal in January 2020 temporarily reduced tariff escalation anxiety; COVID disruptions subsequently reset the demand narrative entirely

historical_cases.name: 2023-2024 Chinese EV Tariff Escalation (EU + US)
historical_cases.peak_move: EU provisional tariffs on Chinese EVs announced mid-2024 (up to ~48% additional); US tariffs on Chinese EVs doubled to 100% under Biden administration May 2024; BYD, SAIC, Geely ADRs and European legacy OEM proxies repriced; supply chain diversification themes for Southeast Asia assembly re-accelerated
historical_cases.exit_trigger: Theme had not fully resolved as of early 2026; partial exit signals included BYD/Stellantis partnership announcements and CATL Morocco/Hungary capacity announcements reducing tariff exposure

differences.description: The 2025-2026 environment features simultaneous, multi-jurisdictional tariff actions — US 100%+ EV tariffs, EU countervailing duties, and potential reciprocal measures — creating a more structurally fragmented supply chain landscape than prior single-country escalations. Historical analogs typically involved one dominant policy actor; the current regime may lock in supply chain bifurcation rather than resolve episodically.

Chinese battery manufacturers (CATL, BYD) have matured into globally cost-competitive suppliers with active ex-China capacity buildouts (Hungary, Morocco, Mexico consideration), meaning tariff-driven supply chain shifts now involve credible alternative geographies rather than pure substitution costs. This may compress the duration of margin dislocation for non-Chinese OEMs compared to earlier cycles.

LFP chemistry's cost trajectory and solid-state battery development timelines are compressing the competitive moat of NMC-focused Western supply chains, introducing technology obsolescence risk that was absent in the 2021-2022 lithium supercycle. Investors in upstream nickel and cobalt-linked supply chains may face structural demand disappointment independent of tariff outcomes.

EV demand growth has visibly decelerated in key Western markets (US, Germany) entering 2025-2026, diverging from the hypergrowth assumptions embedded in 2021-2023 supply chain investment decisions. This demand softness may suppress the urgency of supply chain normalization and extend the overhang of stranded gigafactory capacity announcements.

similarities: As in prior cycles, lithium and critical mineral price volatility remains the primary margin transmission mechanism between upstream supply shocks and OEM profitability, preserving the historical playbook's relevance for upstream miners.

Policy announcement-driven sentiment spikes followed by execution-gap disappointments have been observed in every prior IRA/tariff cycle, suggesting headline-to-reality lag remains a consistent behavioral pattern.

Geographically diversified supply chain restructuring (analogous to post-2018 China+1 electronics manufacturing shift) appears to follow a multi-year capital allocation cycle similar to prior supply chain reshoring waves.
this_time_different.observation: The current EV supply chain shift cycle may be structurally longer and more geographically complex than prior analogs, but the combination of genuine demand deceleration and Chinese OEM cost competitiveness introduces cross-cutting forces that make the ultimate magnitude and beneficiary of supply chain re-routing more uncertain than historical lithium or tariff episodes.

exit_signals: Sustained stabilization or mean-reversion in lithium carbonate/hydroxide spot prices accompanied by visible inventory normalization at cathode manufacturers — historically marked the transition from acute supply chain stress to digestion phase.

Major OEM (Ford, GM, Stellantis, Volkswagen) formal announcement of gigafactory restart, capacity expansion resumption, or upward revision to EV volume targets after a period of deferrals — signals demand-side confidence returning to supply chain investment cycle.

Regulatory resolution or legal challenge outcome on EU/US tariff structures (WTO rulings, bilateral negotiation framework, or formal trade deal announcement) that reduces binary policy uncertainty for supply chain planning.

CATL, LG Energy Solution, or comparable tier-1 cell maker achieving meaningful ex-China production ramp (observed in quarterly shipment data) sufficient to serve tariff-protected markets — signals tariff-driven supply chain anxiety has been operationally addressed rather than remaining speculative.

Visible margin recovery in EV-focused OEM quarterly results (gross margin per vehicle turning positive or expanding sequentially for 2+ quarters) combined with analyst consensus revision cycles turning constructive — historically coincided with end of supply chain cost-shock narrative dominance.
duration_type_reasoning: EV supply chain restructuring is a multi-year structural transition driven by simultaneous technology adoption curves, geopolitical decoupling, and capital-intensive manufacturing buildouts — without a single discrete event that can resolve it, making it an extended rather than bounded or purely derivative theme.

real_world_timeline.description: The current cycle's structural origin may be traced to the convergence of IRA passage (August 2022), peak lithium prices signaling supply chain fragility, and initial EU/US regulatory scrutiny of Chinese EV import competitiveness — which collectively reoriented supply chain investment from pure growth optimization toward tariff resilience and geographic diversification.
